message:
🐛 fix(ui): FAB button clipped to half-circle by scrollbar (#8551)

* 🐛 fix: What's New modal for developer-mode (SHA-based) updates

The modal returned null when latestRelease was absent, which is always
the case on developer channel (SHA-based tracking, no GitHub release
object). The green button rendered but clicking it produced an
invisible modal.

Fix: only return null when both !latestRelease and !isOpen. When isOpen
is true, render the modal with fallback copy ('New commits available'
instead of release tag, generic notes instead of release body).

Also adds a rotating tip about issue-to-fix turnaround time.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 fix(ui): FAB button clipped to half-circle by scrollbar

The floating action button (+ / Console Studio) was clipped to a
half-circle on the right edge because right-6 (24px) didn't account
for the ~15px scrollbar on the main content area.

Bump all three sidebar states:
- No sidebar: right-6 → right-10 (24px → 40px)
- Minimized:  right-[72px] → right-[88px]
- Expanded:   right-[536px] → right-[552px]

The extra 16px provides breathing room for the scrollbar + the
button's own 40px width without overlapping the content edge.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -125,9 +125,12 @@ export function FloatingDashboardActions({
 
   const getPositionClasses = () => {
     if (isMobile) return 'left-4 bottom-4'
-    if (!isSidebarOpen) return 'right-6 bottom-20'
-    if (isSidebarMinimized) return 'right-[72px] bottom-20'
-    return 'right-[536px] bottom-20'
+    // right-10 (40px) instead of right-6 (24px) — the scrollbar on
+    // the main content area eats ~15px of viewport width, so right-6
+    // left the 40px button clipped to a half-circle (#8464 follow-up).
+    if (!isSidebarOpen) return 'right-10 bottom-20'
+    if (isSidebarMinimized) return 'right-[88px] bottom-20'
+    return 'right-[552px] bottom-20'
   }
   const positionClasses = getPositionClasses()
